title: unable to inspect buildkit cache artifacts
body:
I am running [buildkit](https://github.com/moby/buildkit) with container cache export enabled via this flag `--export-cache type=registry,ref=my-registry.com/buildcache/$CI_PROJECT_NAME:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG,ignore-error=true,compression=zstd,compression-level=4` 

This pushes container build-cache layers to nexus. I want to use `skopeo` to clean the caches based on a custom logic. I can list all tags with `skopeo` but inspecting a tag fails because it has an unsupported Media-Type for its config.  

```bash
skopeo inspect docker://my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app:develop
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "docker://my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app:develop": reading manifest develop in my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app: manifest unknown
```

Is it possible to sypport the `application/vnd.buildkit.cacheconfig.v0` MediaType within  `skopeo`?

Example manifest:
```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.buildkit.cacheconfig.v0",
    "digest": "sha256:54ff057e2201ae3e339830a648d193a6cb8613993770155960e910303ee9294b",
    "size": 15033
  },
  "layers": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip",
      "digest": "sha256:0061c2d3f96191f9b5da8b4cea074c1ed338bde4113f469ed1dcb1a002883d9d",
      "size": 324,
      "annotations": {
        "buildkit/createdat": "2025-10-23T11:54:04.733319249Z",
        "containerd.io/distribution.source.my-registry.com": "buildcache/my-app",
        "containerd.io/uncompressed": "sha256:126d8c51caacf95a546c4e2929a09ea37f3196f50590bdb585fdf2ad854f3e8c"
      }
    },
  ...
  ]
}
```

Version-Info:
 * System: alpine 3.22.1
 * skopeo version 1.20.0

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out:

> `reading manifest develop in my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app: manifest unknown`

This does not say anything about a config MIME type; AFAICS it doesn’t get that far, simply because the repo or tag does not exist on the registry. If it does exist, can you start with providing a `--debug` output of that command?

(There is #1933 , and that might well be relevant here, but again the error above means Skopeo doesn’t get that far.)

Here are the debug logs:

```txt
/ # skopeo inspect  docker://my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app:develop --debug
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf"
DEBU[0000] Trying to access "my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app:develop"
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app found in /run/containers/0/auth.json
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app found in /root/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app found in /root/.docker/config.json
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app found in /root/.dockercfg
DEBU[0000] No credentials for my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app found
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for my-registry.com/buildcache/my-app:develop, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore    
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/my-registry.com
DEBU[0000] GET https://my-registry.com/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://my-registry.com/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://my-registry.com/v2/token?scope=repository%3Abuildcache%2Fmy-app%3Apull&service=https%3A%2F%2Fmy-registry.com%2Fv2%2Ftoken      
DEBU[0000] Increasing token expiration to: 60 seconds
DEBU[0000] GET https://my-registry.com/v2/buildcache/my-app/manifests/develop
DEBU[0000] Content-Type from manifest GET is "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"
FATA[0000] unsupported image-specific operation on artifact with type "application/vnd.buildkit.cacheconfig.v0"
```

If I can somehow help, let me know.

Thanks, yes, that’s #1933.

For now, `skopeo inspect --raw` shows the raw information.
